webpage_section.section_description: Unordered list container holding all card items in a grid layout

difference_1.computed_style_change: display: grid vs block; grid-template-columns: 368px 368px 368px vs none; column-gap: 16px vs normal; row-gap: 16px vs normal
difference_1.difference: The original uses CSS Grid layout with three equal columns (368px each) and 16px gaps between cards. The migrated version uses block display with no grid, causing the cards to stack or flow differently. The original shows 2 visible cards side by side in a neat grid, while the migrated shows 3 cards in a horizontal row without the grid structure.

difference_2.computed_style_change: display: flex vs block; flex-direction: column vs row; border-radius: 8px vs 0px; background-color: rgb(251,177,117) vs transparent
difference_2.difference: The original first card has a vertical flex layout with rounded corners and orange background. The migrated card has no rounded corners and transparent background, with a horizontal layout structure instead.

difference_3.difference: The first card's text content area has no top padding in the migrated version (original: 20px), making text sit flush against the image.

difference_4.computed_style_change: display: flex vs block; flex-direction: column vs row; border-radius: 8px vs 0px
difference_4.difference: The original second card has vertical flex layout with rounded corners. The migrated card has no rounded corners and block display.

difference_5.difference: The second card's text content area has no top padding in the migrated version.
